Title: PLY (Python Lex-Yacc) - David Beazley
URL: https://www.dabeaz.com/ply/ply.html
Content: PLY consists of two separate modules; lex.py and yacc.py, both of which are found in a Python package called ply. ... For example: parser.parse(text,lexer=lexer).

Title: [PDF] Writing a Language Interpreter with Python Lex-Yacc (PLY)
URL: https://runtux.com/files/download/parsing-pycon25.4.pdf
Content: • Expressions become nested python functions. • Compile each line into one or multiple statements. • There is a dict to look up next line

Title: PLY (Python Lex-Yacc)
URL: https://www.eng.utah.edu/~cs3100/lectures/l14/ply-3.4/doc/ply.html
Content: # PLY (Python Lex-Yacc). This document provides an overview of lexing and parsing with PLY. PLY-3.0 is compatible with both Python 2 and Python 3. Be aware that Python 3 support is new and has not been extensively tested (although all of the examples and unit tests pass under Python 3.0). If you are using Python 2, you should try to use Python 2.4 or newer. PLY is a pure-Python implementation of the popular compiler construction tools lex and yacc. Thus, if you've used yacc in another programming language, it should be relatively straightforward to use PLY. The rest of this document assumes that you are somewhat familar with parsing theory, syntax directed translation, and the use of compiler construction tools such as lex and yacc in other programming languages. The lex.py module is used to break input text into a collection of tokens specified by a collection of regular expression rules.

Title: PLY (Python Lex-Yacc) - Computer Science
URL: https://www.cs.vu.nl/~jansa/ftp/BK0/ply.html
Content: # PLY (Python Lex-Yacc). PLY is a Python-only implementation of the popular compiler construction tools lex and yacc. However, the overall flavor of the implementation is more closely modeled after the C version of lex and yacc. The other significant feature of PLY is that it provides extensive input validation and error reporting--much more so than other Python parsing tools. Because PLY was primarily developed as an instructional tool, you will find it to be *MUCH* more picky about token and grammar rule specification than most other Python parsing tools. yacc.py is used to recognize language syntax that has been specified in the form of a context free grammar.

Title: PLY (Python Lex-Yacc) — ply 4.0 documentation
URL: https://ply.readthedocs.io
Content: # PLY (Python Lex-Yacc)¶. PLY requires the use of Python 3.6 or greater. PLY is a 100% Python implementation of the lex and yacc tools commonly used to write parsers and compilers. Parsing is based on the same LALR(1) algorithm used by many yacc tools. > * PLY provides *very* extensive error reporting and diagnostic information to assist in parser construction. > * PLY can be used to build parsers for “real” programming languages. Although it is not ultra-fast due to its Python implementation, PLY can be used to parse grammars consisting of several hundred rules (as might be found for a language like C). - Debugging the lex() and yacc() commands. For a detailed overview of parsing theory, consult the excellent book “Compilers : Principles, Techniques, and Tools” by Aho, Sethi, and Ullman. The topics found in “Lex & Yacc” by Levine, Mason, and Brown may also be useful. The GitHub page for PLY can be found at:. # ply.
